name: architecture-enforcer
description: Architecture rule enforcement tool - validates module boundaries, layer dependencies, naming conventions, and structural integrity. Supports Harness Engineering with agent-friendly linting and 6-layer architecture model.
author: jojaeyong

commands.name: arch-check
commands.description: Check codebase against architecture rules and report violations

commands.name: arch-init
commands.description: Initialize architecture rules interactively for a project

agents.name: boundary-checker
agents.description: Analyze imports and dependencies against defined module boundary rules

agents.name: architecture-auditor
agents.description: Deep audit of architecture health, coupling metrics, and improvement suggestions

agents.name: harness-linter
agents.description: Agent-friendly architecture linter with actionable fix instructions for LLMs and automated systems

tools.name: check_layer_violation
tools.description: Check if an import violates layer dependency rules

tools.name: get_layer_rules
tools.description: Get the layer configuration and allowed imports for a file or module

tools.name: check_module_boundary
tools.description: Verify a cross-module dependency is allowed by boundary rules

tools.name: suggest_correct_layer
tools.description: Suggest which layer a type, function, or file belongs to

tools.name: get_architecture_map
tools.description: Return the full architecture map for the current project

tools.name: lint_file
tools.description: Run harness linting on a single file with agent-friendly output

docs.name: layers
docs.description: 6-layer architecture model reference (Types, Config, Repo, Service, Runtime, UI)
